title: Vessels of Dust
excerpt: We are but columns of air vibrating at frequencies we call memory. In the hollows of our chests, we store the echoes of old room acoustics...
content:
Vessels of Dust

We are but columns of air vibrating at frequencies we call memory. In the hollows of our chests, we store the echoes of old room acoustics, the hum of fluorescent lighting in empty hallways.

We speak in waveforms, transient spikes of laughter quickly decayed by the soft absorption of drywall and carpets. We walk through acoustic thresholds, hoping to find a space with zero resonance—a clean slate, an anechoic chamber where the only sound left is the rhythm of our own blood moving.

But the dust gathers in our ears, acting as a low-pass filter, slowly rounding off the sharp corners of our regrets, leaving only a warm, saturated hum.

title: Subwoofer Sins
excerpt: Low frequency vibrations rattling the windowpanes of this rental flat. 45 Hertz is where the anger sits. I mix the sub-bass so loud...
content:
Subwoofer Sins

Low frequency vibrations rattling the windowpanes of this rental flat. 45 Hertz is where the anger sits.

I mix the sub-bass so loud it masks the squeak of the hardwood floors, masks the hum of the old refrigerator, masks the sound of my own voice wondering what went wrong.

We filter out the mud at 250, but we can't filter out the clutter in our heads. We compress the vocals to make them sit upfront, but some stories are meant to stay buried in the back of the mix.

title: The Geometry of Silence
excerpt: Silence is not the absence of sound; it is the presence of structure. In architectural design, negative space defines the building...
content:
The Geometry of Silence

Silence is not the absence of sound; it is the presence of structure.

In architectural design, negative space defines the building. In mixing, the space between transient peaks defines the dynamics. Without the quiet valley, the high peak is just flat compression, a wall of noise that wears down the ear.

We build walls to create rooms, and we create rooms to isolate sound. But isolation is a myth. The low end always leaks through the walls. The street always creeps into the microphone. True art is not about creating a perfect vacuum; it is about choosing which leaks to keep.
